title: Ronseal One Coat Shed & Fence Pouch Tudor Black 950ml - Mix with Water to Make 5 Litres of Paint for Effortless Coverage and Long-Lasting Protection
features: ☑️LESS PLASTIC: Concentrated Pouch, 90% Less Plastic vs 5L Bucket. One 950ml pouch makes 5L of Shed & Fence Protector.
☑️EASY APPLICATION: can be brushed or sprayed on
☑️DURABLE: long lasting can protect against rain frost, snow and sun, providing all weather protection
☑️LONG LASTING: lasting colour and protection for 2 years
description: Ronseal One Coat Shed & Fence Protection for sheds & fences. Don't waste more time than you need painting your shed and fence. One coat's easy to use, it can be brushed or sprayed on and it'll stand up to rain, frost, snow and sun, meaning you'll get lasting colour and protection for 2 years. One 950ml pouch makes 5L of Shed & Fence Protector. Before you start, wait for a warm, dry day (above 10c and not going to rain). Cold, wet weather stops the stain from drying which could make it wash off. Give the wood a good clean with a stiff brush to get rid of any dirt or the stain might not soak in properly. If your fence is grey and hasn't been treated before, you'll need to give the wood a good clean or the stain might not stick. You could clean the wood with either a pressure washer (be careful not to damage the wood) or use a suitable fence cleaner. The wood will need to be dry before you paint it. Cover anything you don't want to get stain on. To check you're happy with the colour, find a small hidden patch of wood to test on before you paint our whole shed or fence. To mix, pour the pouch into a bucket. Fill the pouch with water, reseal & shake. Pouch holds 1 litre. Pour the pouch of water into the bucket. Mix well for 1 minute. For the first pour, add water gradually like making a cake. Add 3 more pouches of water by repeating each step, 3 times. Scrape bottom of bucket when mixing between pours. If you're using a brush, get lots of stain on your brush, then brush on a thick coat. Brush in any drips. Wipe any drips off plants or paving with soapy water before it dries. If you're using a sprayer, ensure it's not too windy. Mix the stain in a bucket and pour into the tank, pump it up and start spraying. Use long, seeping, horizontal arm movements and move along the full width of the panel. Drop down and do the same again, overlapping by about half. Clean any overspray with soapy water before it dries.
